name: The System-on-Chip Era
notableFeature: CPU, GPU, modem, and ISP on one die
description: Integrating discrete CPU, GPU, modem, image signal processor, and memory controller onto a single piece of silicon eliminated the inter-chip buses that wasted power and board area. Qualcomm's Snapdragon 820 even brought its own custom CPU microarchitecture (Kryo) in-house rather than licensing a stock ARM design. The tension was integration complexity: cramming more heterogeneous compute blocks onto one die pushed process nodes to their limits and made yield management — getting enough defect-free chips per wafer — an engineering discipline as important as the architecture itself.
